Title: Moonlighting Arabidopsis molybdate transporter 2 family and GSH-complex formation facilitate molybdenum homeostasis

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MOT2 family transporter; major facilitator superfamily; contains four MOT2-specific conserved motifs
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plasma membrane; also co-localizes with cis-Golgi marker
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Global expression in roots (vascular tissue), epidermis and vascular tissue of shoots, young leaves, and developing flowers with strong signals in ovaries; induced by molybdate abundance in all organs except flower
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root vascular tissue; shoot epidermis and vascular tissue; young leaves and main leaf venation; ovaries of developing flowers; induced by molybdate supply
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Molybdenum/molybdate available for molybdenum cofactor (Moco)-dependent metabolism
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves; reproductive organs; developing seeds/ovaries; vacuole as storage compartment for molybdate in plant context
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports Moco biosynthesis required for nitrate assimilation and other Moco-enzyme functions such as ABA biosynthesis and sulfite detoxification
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Molybdate availability versus deprivation in hydroponics
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes plant-wide molybdate distribution, cellular import, and supply to Moco biosynthesis; loss reduces nitrate reductase activity by about 30% even under molybdate availability
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast molybdate transport assay; T-DNA knockout analysis; molybdate uptake assay; nitrate reductase activity assay; subcellular localization; BiFC; split-luciferase
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MOT2.1 is a bona fide molybdate transporter demonstrated in a heterologous Moco-yeast assay. 2. It localizes mainly to the plasma membrane and interacts with molybdenum-insertase Cnx1, supporting direct delivery of molybdate to Moco biosynthesis. 3. mot2.1 knockout reduces nitrate reductase activity, indicating impaired molybdate supply for Moco-dependent metabolism. 4. Expression pattern suggests a major role in plant-wide molybdate distribution, especially in young leaves and reproductive tissues.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Single knockout showed no major vegetative growth defect, but combined loss with mot1.1 and mot1.2 drastically reduced nitrate reductase activity and plant survival
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors propose moonlighting function because MOT2.1 was also previously implicated as a Golgi S-adenosyl-methionine transporter (GoSAMT2) for polysaccharide methylation

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MOT2 family transporter; major facilitator superfamily; splice variant with four MOT2-specific conserved motifs
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plasma membrane; also co-localizes with cis-Golgi marker
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Flower-associated expression; mot2.2 promoter active in pollen grains of mature flowers
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Molybdenum/molybdate available for reproductive organ metabolism and Moco-related functions
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Reproductive organs, especially pollen; possibly developing seeds indirectly
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports molybdate supply in reproductive tissues, potentially for ABA-related seed processes and pollen-associated phosphate homeostasis
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Molybdate availability versus deprivation; expression pattern not altered by molybdate availability
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions as a molybdate importer and interacts with Cnx1, but single knockout showed no evident effect on vegetative growth or nitrate reductase activity
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast molybdate transport assay; promoter-GUS expression analysis; subcellular localization; BiFC
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MOT2.2A is a functional molybdate transporter in yeast. 2. It localizes to the plasma membrane and interacts with Cnx1. 3. Its expression is highly specific to pollen grains, suggesting a reproductive role rather than a major vegetative role. 4. Single knockout did not measurably alter nitrate reductase activity in vegetative tissue.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious vegetative growth penalty reported for single knockout
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors suggest MOT2.2A may supply molybdate to Moco biosynthesis in reproductive tissues, possibly supporting AAO-dependent ABA biosynthesis; also part of a moonlighting family previously linked to Golgi SAM transport

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MOT2 family transporter; major facilitator superfamily; shorter splice variant lacking first 41 amino acids
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plasma membrane; also co-localizes with cis-Golgi marker
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Inferred from mot2.2 promoter activity in pollen grains of mature flowers
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Likely pollen grains of mature flowers via mot2.2 promoter
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potential supply of free molybdate in reproductive tissues
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a molybdate transporter in yeast but does not interact with Cnx1, suggesting a role distinct from direct Moco-biosynthesis supply
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast molybdate transport assay; subcellular localization; BiFC
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MOT2.2B is a functional molybdate transporter in yeast. 2. It localizes to the plasma membrane. 3. Unlike MOT2.1, MOT2.2A, and MOT2.3, it did not interact with Cnx1, implying a different physiological role. 4. Authors propose it may supply free molybdate for pollen/seed functions rather than direct Moco biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Proposed by authors to provide free molybdate for purple acid phosphatase inhibition and seed nutrient storage; also belongs to moonlighting MOT2/GoSAMT family

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MOT2 family transporter; major facilitator superfamily; contains four MOT2-specific conserved motifs
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plasma membrane; also co-localizes with cis-Golgi marker
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports molybdate supply in reproductive organs, potentially contributing to seed loading and Moco-related metabolism
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Molybdate availability versus deprivation; expression pattern not affected by molybdate
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions as a molybdate importer and interacts with Cnx1, but single knockout showed no detectable effect on vegetative growth or nitrate reductase activity
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast molybdate transport assay; promoter-GUS expression analysis; subcellular localization; BiFC
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MOT2.3 is a functional molybdate transporter in yeast. 2. It localizes to the plasma membrane and interacts with Cnx1. 3. Expression in ovaries suggests a role in reproductive molybdate allocation, possibly seed loading. 4. No evident vegetative phenotype or nitrate reductase defect was seen in the single knockout.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious vegetative growth penalty reported for single knockout
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors suggest a role in loading molybdate into developing seeds; also part of the moonlighting MOT2 family previously associated with Golgi SAM transport
